coredata.dc:title: Highly sensitive and selective colorimetric sensor for thiocyanate based on electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation reaction with Gold nanostars etching
coredata.dc:creator: Cui, Xinwen
Wei, Tianxiang
Hao, Mengyuan
Qi, Qi
Wang, Huafeng
Dai, Zhihui
coredata.dc:description: In this work, we developed an electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy for highly sensitive and selective detection of thiocyanate (SCN−). Gold nanostars (AuNSs) with uniform and sharp tips were first prepared, and we found they can be quickly etched to gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) under electrochemical oxidation with the existence of halide and halogen-like ions. Through introducing SCN−-selective molecule: zinc phthalocyanine (ZnPc), the fabricated ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode can rapidly and selectively response to SCN− under electrochemical oxidation, manifesting as a noticeable change in color from navy blue to red. Thus SCN− concentration can be easily reflected. The wide wavelength tuning range of AuNSs to AuNPs make the ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO sensor obtain a much wider detection range for SCN− (10 nM to 80 mM) than most other reported studies. In addition, the detection limit is as low as 3 nM. It renders the sensor to be easily used in much diluted matrixes, which can further lower the interference. We further applied the colorimetric sensor to SCN− detection in wastewater and milk, excellent performance was obtained. The proposed electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy will have good promise in developing colorimetric sensors with high selectivity and wide detection range, and will display more useful application in environmental monitoring.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Thiocyanate
Colorimetric
Gold nanostar
Electrochemical oxidation
Etching
Complexation

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: absattachment articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table e-component body acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast footnotes grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst misctext orcid primabst ref specialabst

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Morphology characterization of AuNSs etching and feasibility test of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted etching system
Highly sensitive and selective colorimetric sensing performance of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted AuNSs etching system
Real sample analysis of the proposed colorimetric sensor

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Schematic illustration of the proposed method for SCN− detection.

TEM images of AuNSs at different etching degrees in the presence of four concentrations of SCN−: (A) 0, (B) 1 μM, (3) 1 mM, (D) 100 mM. Insets are the corresponding color photos of ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode.
Optimization of (A) the amount of ZnPc-AuNSs dropped on the electrode surface, (B) the high potential value of cycle voltammetry technique, (C) cycle voltammetry scan time.
(A) UV-vis absorption spectra obtained in test solution with different SCN− concentrations. (B) Calibration curve for A543nm/A798nm value versus SCN− concentration.

Detection of SCN− using ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode in the presence of other interfering ions. (a) Ca2+ + SCN−, (b) Mg2+ + SCN−, (c) SO4 2− + SCN−, (d) NO3 − + SCN−, (e) Na+ + SCN−, (f) Cl− + SCN−, (g) Br− + SCN−, (h) I− + SCN−, (i) Fe3+ + SCN−, (j) SCN−. The concentration of SCN− is 10−4 M, and the concentration of the other ions is 10-3 M.
Comparison of the detection range and limit for different SCN− analytical methods.
Detection results obtained by using our proposed colorimetric sensor.

head.ce:title: Highly sensitive and selective colorimetric sensor for thiocyanate based on electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation reaction with Gold nanostars etching

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: Jiangsu Collaborative Innovation Centre of Biomedical Functional Materials and Jiangsu key Laboratory of Biofunctional Materials, School of Chemistry and Materials Science, Nanjing Normal University, Nanjing, 210023, People’s Republic of China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Jiangsu Collaborative Innovation Centre of Biomedical Functional Materials and Jiangsu key Laboratory of Biofunctional Materials
School of Chemistry and Materials Science, Nanjing Normal University

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: The two authors contribute equally to this article.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this work, we developed an electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy for highly sensitive and selective detection of thiocyanate (SCN−). Gold nanostars (AuNSs) with uniform and sharp tips were first prepared, and we found they can be quickly etched to gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) under electrochemical oxidation with the existence of halide and halogen-like ions. Through introducing SCN−-selective molecule: zinc phthalocyanine (ZnPc), the fabricated ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode can rapidly and selectively response to SCN− under electrochemical oxidation, manifesting as a noticeable change in color from navy blue to red. Thus SCN− concentration can be easily reflected. The wide wavelength tuning range of AuNSs to AuNPs make the ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO sensor obtain a much wider detection range for SCN− (10 nM to 80 mM) than most other reported studies. In addition, the detection limit is as low as 3 nM. It renders the sensor to be easily used in much diluted matrixes, which can further lower the interference. We further applied the colorimetric sensor to SCN− detection in wastewater and milk, excellent performance was obtained. The proposed electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy will have good promise in developing colorimetric sensors with high selectivity and wide detection range, and will display more useful application in environmental monitoring.

ce:section.ce:para: Thiocyanate (SCN−), a pseudohalogen, which mainly comes from the following fields: textile dyeing, hydrometallurgy, electroplating and photofinishing in industrial processes, as well as cigarette smoke in everyday life and car exhaust (Chandler et al., 2018; Chen et al., 2017; Cho et al., 2018; Li et al., 2018a,2018b; Pienpinijtham et al., 2011; Seetasang and Kaneta, 2019; Wang et al., 2010; Yang et al., 2019; Zhi et al., 2015), is a widespread anion pollutant in water and has a serious impact to aquatic plants and animals due to the cumulative environmental effect. Also, as SCN− is a component of lactoperoxidase system, it is often used as an antioxidant additive (Banerjee et al., 2013; Peng et al., 2017) in antibacterial and preservative fields, which leads to its present in foods such as milk and cheese. Studies have reported that excess SCN− can adversely affect the host cells (Song et al., 2016) through blocking the uptaking of iodide by the thyroid gland, resulting in the thyroxine concentration decrease and iodine deficiency, and eventually inducing thyroid enlargement (Ankudze et al., 2019; Lu et al., 2015). In addition, SCN− in water and foods can be transformed into highly toxic cyanides (CN−) by way of radiation and chlorination, causing serious environmental problems and human health hazards (Zhao et al., 2015). Therefore, the detection of SCN− is crucial in the investigation of the environment, medicine and life science.

Up to now, many methods for determination of SCN− have been proposed, such as electrospray-ion mobility spectrometry (ESI-IMS) (Jafari and Javaheri, 2010), gas chromatography (Toraño and van Kan, 2003), electrophoresis (Glatz et al., 2001), electrochemistry (Afkhami et al., 2014; Li et al., 2018a,2018b; Ponnaiah et al., 2018), chromatography (Ma et al., 2013a), colorimetric analysis (Gavrilenko et al., 2017, 2019; Ma et al., 2013b; Pena-Pereira et al., 2016), fluorescence spectrometry (Zhao et al., 2018) and so on. However, these methods exist some drawbacks, including time consuming, complicated and cumbersome to operate, requiring expensive instruments and skillful technical operators. Therefore, it is very important to propose a convenient, simple and sensitive SCN− detection method. The advantages of colorimetric analysis methods are simple and rapid, which had attracted more and more attention for researchers (Bustamante et al., 2019; Cheng et al., 2018). The phenomenon of the complexation of iron ions with SCN− to produce blood red complex Fe(SCN)3 has been utilized for developing many colorimetric SCN− analysis methods (Chaterjee, 2015;Nyasulu and Barlag, 2011; Soni et al., 2011). However, these above colorimetric assays usually suffer the disadvantages of the narrow detection range and the high detection limit. Therefore, to develop new colorimetric SCN− analysis methods with simultaneous possessed wide detection range and low detection limit is the pursuit of our ideals.

Gold nanomaterials are widely applied in colorimetric assays due to its strong localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) with a broad range from visible to near-infrared ray region, and the eminent extinction coefficient (Yuan et al., 2012). There are various shapes of gold nanomaterials, such as gold nanoparticles (AuNPs), gold nanorods (AuNRs), gold nanostars (AuNSs), and gold nanocages (AuNCs) (Sudhakar and Santhosh, 2017). Among them, AuNSs own the multi-branched nanostructures with a central core and a number of prominent arms with sharp tips (Niu et al., 2015), which make the local field strength near the tip of the AuNSs can be improved by several orders of magnitude compared to incident light, thus providing a broad development for plasma-enhanced spectroscopy. In addition, the LSPR of AuNSs can be easily adjusted to the near-infrared (NIR) region. Comparing with the most commonly used colloidal AuNPs, which possess an absorption band at about 520 nm, AuNSs has a significant promise for many applications (Niu et al., 2015). Recently, a series of methods for detecting analytes were proposed inspired by the principle of nanocrystals’ etching (Lu et al., 2018), which is displayed as the change of the LSPR and visualized color variance. The etching of nanocrystals has become more and more widely studied in colorimetric sensing (Hauwiller et al., 2018, 2019). Considering the large difference of absorption band between AuNSs and AuNPs (about 200 nm) and the light amplification effect owned by the sharp tips structure, therefore, we suspect that the etching of AuNSs might be developed to be used in colorimetric sensing to widen the detection range and improve the sensitivity.

Herein, by taking advantage of electrochemical oxidation and complexation reaction, we successfully constructed a highly sensitive and selective colorimetric sensor for SCN− detection based on electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation reaction with AuNSs etching. As SCN− has a linear structure with carbon atoms in the center. It can act as a donor function through an S atom to form covalent compounds and complexes with soft metal ions such as gold and silver (Li et al., 2012). Metal phthalocyanines as anionic ionophores can exhibit specific metal-ligand interactions and cause anion selectivity in the membrane and can be used as active materials due to their ionic discrimination ability (Amini et al., 1999). Through a SCN− selective molecular (Zinc phthalocyanine, ZnPc) (Xu et al., 2006) functionalization, the double restrictions of combination of ZnPc and gold nanastar, the fabricated sensor showed high selectivity toward thiocyanate. At first, the synthesized AuNSs were quickly electrochemical oxidized to gold ions at the surface of ITO electrode, and soluble Au thiocyanate was formed in the presence of SCN−, thus inducing AuNSs etching and resulting in changes in the morphology (from star to irregular and finally sphere) and color change (from navy blue to purple-red and finally red). By measuring the ultraviolet absorption spectrum of the ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode, the concentration of SCN− can be quantitatively detected. The ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO sensor exhibited remarkable selectivity, sensitivity and visualization towards SCN− analysis under electrochemical oxidization. The long wavelength of AuNSs and the light amplification effect produced by the sharp tips structure also endow the colorimetric sensor with a much wider detection range (10 nM to 80 mM) and lower detection limit (3 nM). Also, the selection of ITO electrode as the working electrode let the assay can be easily and clearly visualized. Comparing with other electrodes, the ITO electrode has the advantages of low cost and easily tailorable surface working area, which is more appropriate for in-situ field monitoring of environmental pollution. Therefore, the proposed electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy is expected to provide a basis for more useful application in environmental monitoring. The colorimetric assay process is illustrated in Scheme 1 .

Chloroauric acid trihydrate (HAuCl4·3H2O, 99%), chitosan and polyvinylpyrrolidone PVP, powder, average MW∼29,000 were acquired from Sigma-Aldrich (USA). Sodium citrate (99%), potassium thiocyanate (KSCN), sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF) were obtained from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (China). ZnPc was purchased from Aladdin Reagent Co., Ltd. (China). All solutions, unless special mentioned, were prepared with ultrapure water (18.2 MΩ cm) purified by a Millipore ultrapure water system (MilliQ, USA). Indium tin oxide (ITO) glass used as work electrode was purchased from Jintan Kondrk Photoelectric Science & Technology Co. Ltd. (China).

Cyclic voltammetry (CV) experiment was performed on a CHI 660D electrochemical workstation (CH instruments, China). The ultraviolet and visible absorption spectroscopy (UV–vis) was performed at room temperature on a Thermo Scientific Multiskan GO microplate reader (Thermo Fisher, USA) with the wavelength range from 400 to 1000 nm. Transmission electron microscopes (TEM) images were taken by a JEOL-2100 F HRTEM microscope (JEOL, Japan).

Firstly, the gold seeds were prepared according to the previously described method (Liu and Lu, 2006). In particular, 15 mL of 1% citrate solution was quickly added into 100 mL of HAuCl4 boiling solution (1 mM) at the condition of vigorous stirring and kept boiling for 15 min. The obtained wine red solution was chilled down at room temperature, and was stored at 4 °C before usage. The morphology of the synthesized Au seeds was observed by TEM. It can be seen from Fig. S1 that the Au seeds have a uniform spherical structure with an average diameter of about 20 nm. The other characterization towards Au seeds using UV–vis absorption spectroscopy showed that the absorption peak was located at 525 nm (Fig. S2). These results confirmed that the Au seeds were successfully synthesized. Then, the AuNSs were prepared according to the reported procedures (Kumar et al., 2008; Rodríguez-Lorenzo et al., 2012). In detail, 0.7 mL of 2.5 mM PVP solution were mixed with the already produced gold seeds solutions. Then the mixture was kept stirring for 1.5 h at room temperature. The synthesized gold seeds connected with PVP were purified by centrifugation at 11,000 rpm for 15 min to move away the unconnected PVP. After then, the precipitates were re-dissolved in 100 μL of alcohol. Subsequently, aqueous solution of HAuCl4 (41 μL, 100 mM) was blended with PVP (15 mL, 10 mM), immediately followed by speedy injection of 100 μL of PVP@ gold seeds under incessant mixing. Accompanying the solution color transformed from pale pink to blue black, the AuNSs were successfully prepared. Finally, In order to remove the superfluous reactants and solvents, the product was centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 30 min and washed by water for three times.

The prepared AuNSs were firstly dissolved with 100 μL of water, 50 μL of 0.05 mg mL−1 ZnPc solution and 50 μL of 0.25% chitosan (CS) solution were added to react for 1 h at room temperature. Then 20 μL of the above mixture was dropped on the pretreated ITO electrode, and infrared lamp was used for drying. The pretreatment process of ITO electrode can follow the steps below: the ITO electrodes were ultrasonic treatment with acetone/ethanol mixed solution (v/v = 1:1, containing 1 M NaOH), and then washed with ultrapure water several times according to the previous reported research (Zhu et al., 2016); next the ITO electrode were dried in 60 °C oven for following use. The dried ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode can be used directly in the next experiment.

The electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation reaction with AuNSs etching was carried out by cyclic voltammetry in a series of different concentrations of KSCN solution at the potential voltage range of 0−2 V (vs. SCE) by using the constructed ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Morphology characterization of AuNSs etching and feasibility test of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted etching system
ce:section.ce:para: With the increase of SCN− concentration in electrolyte, the AuNSs were expected to be electrochemical oxidized to etch to deeper degrees, and more obvious color change could be seen on ITO electrode. By using TEM, we investigated the morphology change of AuNSs. The AuNSs were etched to different degrees in electrolyte containing different SCN− concentrations and the corresponding color photos are shown in Fig. 1 . As shown in Fig. 1A, the synthesized ZnPc-AuNSs have a star-shaped structure with lots of pointed tip. The average diameter of the ZnPc-AuNSs is about 60 nm with good monodispersity and uniform morphological features. When treated with a low concentration of SCN−, as shown in Fig. 1B, the ZnPc-AuNSs began to be etched, the thorn of the tip at the surface became dull, and the AuNSs shaped irregular. Fig. 1C shows the morphology of the ZnPc-AuNSs with the further etching state. We can see as the degree of etching increases, the pointed tips at the surface of AuNSs gradually disappear and the shape tended to be more spherical. Fig. 1D shows that the morphology of ZnPc-AuNSs treated with a large concentration of SCN−. It shows that AuNSs were completely etched into nanospheres with a mean diameter of about 25 nm. The inset photos at the upper right corner of each TEM image correspond to the color change images of ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode corresponding to the AuNSs with different etching degrees. As can be seen from the Fig. 1A inset, the ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode appeared as blue-black color before reaction. When the etching occurs, the color of the ZnPc-AuNSs/ITO electrode changed into blue-violet color (shown in the Fig. 1B inset). When further etched, the color is approach to purple (shown in the Fig. 1C inset). The color of Fig. 1D inset is pink that corresponding to the completely etched AuNPs. These results verified the feasibility of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted etching system. Thus constructing the colorimetric sensor for SCN− determination based on electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation reaction with AuNSs etching is proved to be workable.

To obtain a better sensing performance, the detection conditions including the volume of ZnPc-AuNSs, the high potential value and scan time of cyclic voltammetry technique were optimized. The electrochemical oxidation of AuNSs on ITO electrodes was investigated by cyclic voltammetry with different volumes of ZnPc-AuNSs solution and the result was shown in Fig. 2 A. When the volume of ZnPc-AuNSs solution increased from 10–20 μL, the ratio of the absorption peak at 543 nm and 798 nm (A543nm/A798nm) increased. When the volume of ZnPc-AuNSs solution increased from 20 to 40 μL, A543nm/A798nm decreased. The maximum ratio peak was attained at the volume of 20 μL. The trend between 10–20 μL might due to the fact that when the amount of AuNSs was insufficient than SCN−, AuNSs would be completely etched to AuNPs and even much smaller AuNPs by excess SCN−, thus the absorbance value in 543 nm would become smaller. When the amount of ZnPc-AuNSs solution increased from 20 to 40 μL, AuNSs were excessive, the etching reaction was incompletion, so the A543nm/A798nm decreased with more ZnPc-AuNSs solution. Therefore, the optimal volume of ZnPc-AuNSs solution was determined to be 20 μL.

In this experiment, the AuNSs on the ITO electrode were electrochemically oxidized by cyclic voltammetry, so the experimental parameters of cyclic voltammetry are critical for the sensing performance. Firstly, the high potential value of cyclic voltammetry technique was optimized. As shown in Fig. 2B, with the increase of the high potential, A543nm/A798nm increased with increasing high potential value of cyclic voltammetry technique and tended to level off after 2 V, indicating the AuNSs have been fully oxidized on the electrode surface at 2 V high potential. Thus, 2 V was chosen as the experimental parameter of cyclic voltammetry. Then, the scan time of cyclic voltammetry was optimized as the very important experimental parameter. It can be observed in Fig. 2C that A543nm/A798nm became bigger until the scan time reached to 240 s. Then A543nm/A798nm became smaller with the further increase of the scan time. That means the AuNSs were completely etched to AuNPs at 240 s. When the electrochemical oxidation continued, the AuNPs with large diameter were further etched into AuNPs with smaller diameter, and the absorption peak continued to blue-shift below 543 nm, so the ratio A543nm/A798nm further decreased. Therefore, during the cyclic voltammetry scan, 240 s was chosen as the optimum time for the electrochemical oxidation.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Highly sensitive and selective colorimetric sensing performance of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted AuNSs etching system
ce:section.ce:para: The specificity of the electrochemical oxidation-assisted AuNSs etching system was investigated in the presence of various anions such as SO4 2−, NO3 −, Cl−, Br− and I−, and metallic ions including Ca2+, Mg2+, Na+ and Fe3+. When SCN− was mixed with other ions, it was found that the A543nm/A798nm values were almost the same with the only SCN− (Fig. 4 ). The results confirmed the good selectivity of our proposed colorimetric sensor. Learned from the literature (Xu et al., 2019), SCN− is a stable pseudohalogen with properties similar to halide ions. So many previous researches towards SCN− detection could not draw a distinction between SCN− and halide ions, especially I−. In this work, the good selectivity of our proposed SCN− sensor mainly resulted from the modification of the SCN− selective molecular: ZnPc on the ITO electrode. Comparing with the AuNSs/ITO electrode without modification of ZnPc, the interference of halogen ions (Cl−, Br−, I−) on SCN− detection is obvious (Fig. S3), the greatly improved selectivity obtained through modification of ZnPc to the electrode has been confirmed. These results indicated that the proposed SCN− sensor has a promising prospect in practical application.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Real sample analysis of the proposed colorimetric sensor
ce:section.ce:para: To estimate the practicability of the proposed colorimetric sensor for detecting SCN−, tap water, industrial wastewater, and liquid milk were chosen as the real samples. Before the detection of real samples, we diluted the industrial wastewater, and liquid milk samples 1000 times by ultrapure water. Certain amounts of SCN− ions were used to spike into these real samples. The results in Table 2 showed a good consistency between the addition concentration and the detection concentration of SCN− in real samples. The obtained recoveries were within a range of 93–113 %, demonstrating its good potential value for practical application. Furthermore, we found our colorimetric sensor can be applied in colored wastewater because the colorimetric test is performed on the ITO electrode, not directly using the liquid samples like other colorimetric assays. In brief, our colorimetric SCN− sensor was a promising detector in environmental monitoring.

In summary, an electrochemical oxidation-assisted etching strategy has been presented for the first time. AuNSs with uniform and sharp tips modified with SCN−-selective molecule (ZnPc) can be quickly and selectively etched to AuNPs under electrochemical oxidation with the existence of SCN−. Due to the strong coordination effect between gold ions and SCN−, the absorption peaks of the AuNSs have significantly changed and accompanied by obvious color change. The proposed colorimetric sensor has the advantages of high sensitivity, excellent selectivity, wide detection range, timesaving procedure, and handy operation. We believe that this sensor will be a powerful implement for detecting trace amounts of SCN− in environment such as wastewater and milk. Furthermore, the proposed electrochemical oxidation-assisted complexation strategy is expected to provide a basis for more useful application in environmental monitoring and other analysis fields.

Xinwen Cui: Data curation, Formal analysis, Methodology, Investigation. Tianxiang Wei: Conceptualization, Writing - review & editing. Mengyuan Hao: Data curation. Qi Qi: Validation. Huafeng Wang: Formal analysis. Zhihui Dai: Funding acquisition, Project administration, Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare no competing financial interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21625502, No. 21705079 and No. 21974070), the Natural Science Foundation of Jiangsu Province (BK20171033), and the Natural Science Foundation of the Jiangsu Higher Education Institutions of China (17KJB150026). We appreciate the financial support from the Priority Academic Program Development of Jiangsu Higher Education Institutions and the Program for Jiangsu Collaborative Innovation Center of Biomedical Functional Materials.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary material related to this article can be found, in the online version, at doi:https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhazmat.2020.122217.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: The following is Supplementary data to this article:
